term: pañcupādānakkhandhesu assādo ca ādīnavo ca nissaraṇaṃ ca
literal_meaning: the gratification, danger, and escape in relation to the five clung-to heaps
preferred_translation: the gratification, danger, and escape in relation to the five clung-to heaps
alternative_translations: the allure, drawback, and escape regarding the five clung-to heaps
discouraged_translations: the pleasure, harm, and exit in the five aggregates of attachment
definition: Canonical gratification-danger-escape formula applied to the five clung-to heaps.
notes: Use this record when the text applies the assāda-ādīnava-nissaraṇa framework to the appropriated heaps. The phrase inherits the fixed whole-expression policy for pañcupādānakkhandhā and keeps the triad in one stable voice. Comparative alternates like allure and drawback are allowed in explanation, but the default should stay compact and governed in direct translation.

context_rules.context: In direct doctrinal translation of the formula
context_rules.rendering: the gratification, danger, and escape in relation to the five clung-to heaps

context_rules.context: In explanatory prose unpacking the triad with more familiar alternates
context_rules.rendering: the allure, drawback, and escape regarding the five clung-to heaps
context_rules.notes: Allowed alternate for explanation, not the default.

example_phrases.pali: pañcupādānakkhandhesu assādo ca ādīnavo ca nissaraṇaṃ ca
example_phrases.translation: the gratification, danger, and escape in relation to the five clung-to heaps
example_phrases.notes: Canonical application of the triad to the heap family.
